title: seanpatrickmay.github.io
oneLiner: Static portfolio with live Garmin and Spotify widgets plus project case studies.
cardDescription: Static portfolio with live Garmin + Spotify dashboards and a dedicated project archive.
proofPoints: Static export optimized for GitHub Pages
Scheduled Garmin + Spotify refresh workflows
Project archive + detail pages driven by a JSON catalog
overview: This site is a static-exported Next.js portfolio that combines hand-curated project writeups with lightweight live data feeds. The goal is a fast personal site that still feels current without depending on a permanent backend.
whatIBuilt: Static-exported Next.js site with homepage, project archive, and per-project routes
Data refresh scripts and workflows for Garmin and Spotify summaries
Reusable UI primitives for cards, pills, filters, and long-form project pages
howItWorks: Scheduled scripts write JSON data into public/
Pages import static JSON directly during build time
GitHub Pages serves the exported site as static HTML
results: Fast static site with dynamic-feeling activity widgets
Shareable /projects/ + /projects/<slug>/ routes compatible with static export
nextSteps: Replace manual project metadata entry with a GitHub-informed sync path
Add screenshots and richer evidence to more project pages
bullets: Static-exported Next.js site with a dedicated project archive and detail pages.
Live Garmin and Spotify widgets powered by scheduled JSON refresh scripts.
Structured project metadata model that drives homepage featuring, filters, and case studies.

title: Life-Dashboard
oneLiner: Self-hosted wellness dashboard that turns Garmin biometrics and nutrition logs into actionable daily insights.
featuredDescription: Wellness dashboard turning Garmin + nutrition into actionable insights.
cardDescription: Wellness dashboard turning Garmin + nutrition into actionable insights.
proofPoints: Garmin ingestion + trend modeling pipeline
Agentic nutrition workflow with structured goals and to-dos
Full-stack app: FastAPI + Postgres + React/TS
overview: Life-Dashboard is a self-hosted wellness app that combines Garmin biometrics, nutrition tracking, and AI-generated guidance into a calm, Monet-inspired interface. It is designed to turn raw health data into concrete daily actions rather than passive dashboard views.
whatIBuilt: FastAPI backend for Garmin ingestion, insight generation, and structured wellness workflows
React/Vite frontend for dashboard views, trends, nutrition, and assistant-style interaction
Postgres-backed data model for biometrics, nutrition logs, insights, and to-do generation
howItWorks: Pull Garmin activities and recovery metrics into Postgres
Aggregate recent trends and generate readiness-style summaries with Vertex AI
Surface metrics, recommendations, and nutrition context through a React interface
results: End-to-end wellness pipeline from raw biometrics to actionable outputs
Clean separation between ingestion, modeling, and product UI
nextSteps: Add tighter evals for AI recommendations
Expand training-readiness logic into more personalized planning
bullets: Self-hosted FastAPI + React application for Garmin, nutrition, and wellness workflows.
Agentic pipeline turns health context into summaries, readiness-style insights, and to-dos.
Monet-inspired UI direction gives the product a distinct visual identity beyond a default dashboard.

title: LecteurAide
oneLiner: French reading companion that turns long-form PDFs into scene-based translations, vocabulary, and quizzes.
featuredDescription: French reading companion with translations, vocab, and scene summaries.
cardDescription: French reading companion with translations, vocab, and scene summaries.
proofPoints: Scene segmentation + LLM summaries for long PDFs
Sentence-level translation and vocabulary extraction
FastAPI ingestion API + Next.js reader UI
overview: LecteurAide is a French reading companion for long-form PDFs. It ingests a book, segments it into scenes, translates sentences, extracts vocabulary, and generates comprehension questions before presenting everything in a purpose-built reading interface.
whatIBuilt: FastAPI backend for book upload, scene segmentation, translation, vocabulary extraction, and persistence
Next.js frontend for upload, library browsing, and scene-by-scene reading
Vertex AI and Google Cloud Translation pipeline for turning raw PDFs into guided reading experiences
howItWorks: Upload a French PDF and title via the ingestion API
Segment the text into scenes and annotate it with translations, vocab, and questions
Persist the processed book and render it through the web reader
results: Turns static PDFs into an interactive reading workflow
Combines document ingestion, translation, and reader UX in one end-to-end product
nextSteps: Add screenshots and a richer sample book walkthrough
Expand the reader with stronger review and spaced-repetition loops
bullets: FastAPI ingestion pipeline converts French PDFs into scene-structured reading content.
Sentence-level translation, vocabulary extraction, and comprehension questions create a guided study loop.
Next.js reader UI supports upload, library browsing, and scene-by-scene reading.

title: Human Digit Recognition
oneLiner: Two-stage computer-vision pipeline that detects hands and predicts digit counts from webcam imagery.
featuredDescription: Two-stage hand detection + finger-count classification pipeline with a Flask demo.
cardDescription: Two-stage hand detection + finger-count classification pipeline with a Flask demo.
proofPoints: Two-stage pipeline: hand localization -> digit-count classification
Custom annotation and training workflow
Flask API + browser-facing demo
overview: Human Digit Recognition is a two-stage perception project that detects a hand region in an image and then classifies how many digits are raised. It includes the modeling workflow, annotation tooling, pretrained weights, and a lightweight Flask-based demo path.
whatIBuilt: PyTorch models for hand-box detection and digit-count classification
Annotation tooling and notebooks for training and evaluation
Flask inference server and browser-facing prediction flow
howItWorks: Capture or upload an image frame
Predict a hand bounding box from the full frame
Crop the detected region and run digit classification
Return the predicted count through the Flask API
results: End-to-end perception workflow from annotation to serving
Demonstrates staged CV inference rather than a single-model toy demo
nextSteps: Package dependencies more cleanly for reproducible setup
Add screenshots and clearer example outputs to the repo and portfolio

gallery.caption: Two-stage training setup for detection and classification.

bullets: Two-stage PyTorch pipeline isolates a hand and then predicts the digit count.
Custom annotation workflow and training notebooks support model iteration.
Flask demo path makes the CV pipeline usable from a browser rather than just notebooks.

title: Wildfire Modeling
oneLiner: Research workflow for wildfire data ingestion, GOFER-style conversion, and locational regression modeling.
cardDescription: Wildfire data + regression workflow spanning Earth Engine downloads, normalization, and local modeling.
proofPoints: Earth Engine download and normalization pipeline
Locational spread + continuation regression workflows
Lightweight local viewer for GOFER-style JSON outputs
overview: Wildfire Modeling is a research workflow for collecting wildfire-related geospatial data, converting it into GOFER-style JSON, and running local regression analyses over fire spread and continuation. It sits between raw remote-sensing data and downstream modeling experiments.
whatIBuilt: CLI scripts for downloading GOES confidence stacks and aligned RTMA meteorology
Conversion tools that normalize geospatial outputs into modeling-friendly JSON
Regression workflows and notebooks for single-fire and multi-fire analysis
howItWorks: Download wildfire confidence stacks and aligned meteorology
Convert outputs into GOFER-style JSON time series
Run locational regressions and inspect outputs through notebooks or the local viewer
results: Repeatable research workflow from Earth Engine exports to regression outputs
Supports both single-fire analysis and broader multi-fire experiments
nextSteps: Tighten environment bootstrapping and add clearer example outputs
Expand the multiresolution data path and result visualization
bullets: Research workflow bridges remote-sensing downloads, normalization, and wildfire modeling.
Supports GOFER-style JSON generation, RTMA alignment, and locational logistic regressions.
Includes a lightweight local web viewer for inspecting time-series wildfire data outputs.

title: Sentiment Market Arb
oneLiner: Full-stack prototype for ingesting prediction-market data and scanning for covered-outcome arbitrage.
cardDescription: Prediction-market arbitrage prototype with FastAPI ingestion, Postgres storage, and a React operator console.
proofPoints: Venue ingestion for Kalshi and Polymarket
Canonical sports-event mapping workflow
Arbitrage scanning backend + operator console
overview: Sentiment Market Arb is a full-stack prototype for ingesting venue data from prediction markets, normalizing it into a shared sports-event schema, and scanning for pure arbitrage opportunities across covered outcomes.
whatIBuilt: FastAPI backend with ingestion routes, mappings, quote storage, and arbitrage scan endpoints
Postgres-backed schema for events, markets, mapping candidates, quotes, and arbitrage outputs
React operator console for ingestion, mapping review, and arbitrage inspection
howItWorks: Ingest venue markets and quotes from supported platforms
Map venue-specific markets onto canonical sports events
Store normalized quotes and run arbitrage scans across compatible outcome groups
results: Prototype covers ingestion, normalization, review, and scan flows end-to-end
Provides a concrete full-stack environment for arbitrage research rather than a paper design
nextSteps: Tighten secrets/env documentation and add example outputs
Add more robust verification and benchmark flows for scan quality
bullets: FastAPI + Postgres backend ingests venue data and stores normalized arbitrage context.
Mapping layer links venue-specific markets to canonical sports events before scanning.
React operator console exposes ingestion actions, mapping review, and arbitrage inspection.

title: PokerML
oneLiner: Heads-up NLHE research engine combining range-aware search, abstractions, and heuristic action selection.
cardDescription: Heads-up NLHE engine for search, abstraction, and poker strategy experiments.
proofPoints: Range-aware game-state and equity tooling
Alpha-beta style search over poker nodes
Heuristic bet sizing and abstraction experiments
overview: PokerML is a research-oriented heads-up no-limit hold'em engine that models poker decision making as a configurable search problem. It focuses on making ranges, abstractions, and heuristic action logic easy to inspect and iterate on.
whatIBuilt: Core card, deck, player, and node abstractions for modeling poker states
Search logic combining alpha-beta style pruning with range-aware heuristics
Sample river test harness for quickly validating a solver setup
howItWorks: Represent current stacks, pot, ranges, and board state as a poker node
Generate candidate actions and estimate outcomes through search and rollouts
Use heuristics and abstraction logic to keep the decision tree tractable
results: Created a modifiable research engine for poker strategy experimentation
Makes solver ideas inspectable without needing a full industrial poker stack
nextSteps: Add stronger experiment harnesses and example outputs
Layer in CFR and stronger abstraction strategies
bullets: Research engine models heads-up NLHE with explicit ranges, search nodes, and abstractions.
Alpha-beta style search and Monte Carlo evaluation make the solver ideas tractable enough to iterate on.
Sample river harness provides a compact smoke test for the current solver setup.

title: MBTA Leave Now
oneLiner: Spring Boot commute helper that answers when to leave based on live MBTA predictions.
cardDescription: Spring Boot API and lightweight UI for stop-level leave-now guidance and a fixed office commute.
proofPoints: MBTA prediction-driven leave-now API
Fixed commute leave-by planner with transfer and walking buffers
Spring Boot + Gradle Kotlin DSL practice project
overview: MBTA Leave Now is a learning-focused Spring Boot project that answers a commuter question: when should I leave? It includes both a generic stop-level API and a more opinionated leave-by planner for a fixed commute to RTX BBN.
whatIBuilt: Spring MVC endpoints for stop-level leave guidance and a fixed commute planner
MBTA API client using Java HttpClient and Jackson parsing
Minimal browser UI for entering a deadline and viewing leave-by guidance
howItWorks: Fetch upcoming MBTA predictions and schedules for relevant stops
Apply walk-time and transfer buffers to compute a leave-by recommendation
Expose results through a REST API and a lightweight static frontend
results: Concrete practice project for Gradle Kotlin DSL, Spring MVC, and test structure
Bridges backend API logic and a usable frontend in one small repo
nextSteps: Generalize the fixed commute planner into configurable routes
Add example responses and screenshots for easier onboarding
bullets: Spring Boot app uses live MBTA predictions to compute leave-now guidance.
Includes a fixed commute planner that models transfer and walking buffers.
Good small-scale full-stack project for Java, Gradle, and HTTP API practice.

title: study-buddy
oneLiner: Forked study workflow that turns notes and decks into flashcards, exports, and cheat sheets.
cardDescription: Forked agentic study workflow for flashcards, Anki exports, and cheat sheets.
proofPoints: Document ingestion -> flashcards -> cheat-sheet workflow
RAG-backed study flow using ChromaDB
FastAPI app with a focused single-page interface
overview: study-buddy is a forked study companion that turns lecture notes, research papers, and Anki exports into curated flashcards, decks, and cheat sheets. It combines LLM tooling, retrieval, and a simple web interface into one study workflow.
whatIBuilt: Maintained and documented a forked workflow for study-material ingestion and artifact generation
FastAPI-backed flow for converting source material into flashcards and cheat sheets
RAG path using ChromaDB to keep generations anchored in the uploaded material
howItWorks: Upload notes, papers, or deck exports
Index material into a retrieval layer
Generate flashcards, exports, and cheat-sheet style outputs from the grounded context
results: Prototype shows a concrete AI-assisted study loop from raw notes to review artifacts
nextSteps: Document fork-specific contributions more precisely
Add screenshots and example outputs to support portfolio presentation

gallery.caption: High-level workflow across ingestion, retrieval, and generation.

bullets: Forked study workflow turns notes and decks into flashcards, exports, and cheat sheets.
Uses retrieval-backed generation with ChromaDB to keep outputs grounded in source material.
FastAPI service and simple UI make the workflow usable outside a notebook setting.

title: Counterfactual Regret Exploration
oneLiner: Notebook-based exploration of Counterfactual Regret Minimization on simple imperfect-information games.
cardDescription: CFR notebooks exploring regret updates and toy imperfect-information games.
proofPoints: CFR notebooks for rock-paper-scissors and AKQ poker-style games
Learning-focused bridge from toy games to larger poker work
Notebook commentary that preserves the reasoning trail
overview: CFR-Exploration is a small notebook-first repo for understanding Counterfactual Regret Minimization through toy imperfect-information games. It captures the experimentation path and intuition-building that later informed larger poker work.
whatIBuilt: Notebooks exploring CFR on rock-paper-scissors and simplified poker-style games
Commentary and experiments around regret updates and average-strategy behavior
A learning bridge between introductory CFR material and larger poker engines
howItWorks: Open the notebooks and run CFR iterations on toy games
Inspect strategy outputs and convergence behavior
Use the experiments to build intuition before scaling the ideas up
results: Created a compact learning repo for imperfect-information game strategy experiments
nextSteps: Add dependency pinning and exported result artifacts
Summarize notebook outcomes more explicitly in the repo and portfolio
bullets: Notebook-first exploration of CFR on simple imperfect-information games.
Useful bridge between introductory regret-matching exercises and larger poker projects.
Preserves the experimentation trail instead of only the final cleaned-up implementation.

title: N-Queens Genetic Algorithm
oneLiner: Compact genetic algorithm solver for evolving conflict-free N-Queens boards.
cardDescription: Genetic algorithm solver for evolving conflict-free N-Queens boards.
proofPoints: Tournament selection + single-point crossover
Mutation schedule that scales with board size
CLI/.env configurable experiments
overview: This project explores the N-Queens puzzle through a compact genetic algorithm. It is intentionally small and transparent, making it easy to inspect how population size, mutation behavior, and iteration caps affect convergence.
whatIBuilt: Single-file genetic algorithm implementation with tournament selection and crossover
Collision-based fitness function for scoring candidate boards
CLI and .env configuration for quick experimentation
howItWorks: Initialize a population of candidate boards
Score conflicts and select parents via tournaments
Apply crossover and mutation across generations
Stop when a zero-collision solution is found or iteration cap is hit
results: Good compact reference implementation for an evolutionary search workflow
nextSteps: Add seeded benchmarking and clearer example outputs
Try alternate encodings or hybrid local-search ideas
bullets: Compact GA solver keeps the evolutionary search mechanics easy to read and modify.
Tournament selection, crossover, and mutation provide a clear baseline for experimentation.
Useful small repo for combinatorial optimization rather than a generic algorithm toy.

title: Maze-Game
oneLiner: Interactive maze generator and solver with DFS/BFS playback and manual player mode in Java.
cardDescription: Java maze generator/solver with BFS/DFS playback and an interactive player mode.
proofPoints: Maze generation via weighted graph + spanning tree ideas
BFS and DFS solver visualization
Interactive player mode with keyboard controls
overview: Maze-Game is a Java world program that generates solvable mazes, animates breadth-first or depth-first search, and supports a manual play mode. It combines graph generation, search visualization, and event-driven interaction in one compact project.
whatIBuilt: Maze generator over a grid graph using weighted edges and union-find style logic
Solver playback for BFS and DFS so pathfinding is visible instead of abstract
Manual mode with movement, collision checks, and win detection
howItWorks: Generate a maze from weighted connections across a grid
Choose BFS or DFS to visualize a solver path
Optionally switch into manual mode and play through the maze
results: Good early project for graph algorithms, search, and interactive Java state
nextSteps: Add screenshots or GIFs of generation and solver playback
Document the course-library setup more precisely
bullets: Interactive Java project combines maze generation, search visualization, and manual play.
BFS and DFS modes make algorithm differences easy to see on the same generated maze.
Nice compact example of graph logic, world state, and event-driven UI in one file.

title: Hex Reversi
oneLiner: Extensible hex-grid Reversi with GUI, modular strategies, and adapter support.
cardDescription: Hex-grid Reversi with GUI, MVC architecture, and pluggable strategies.
proofPoints: Hex-grid Reversi engine with configurable board shapes
MVC architecture with pluggable views/controllers
Composable AI strategies (minimax + heuristics)
overview: Hex Reversi is an extensible Reversi/Othello implementation on a hexagonal grid. The core emphasis is software design: clean MVC separation, adapters for different play modes, and modular AI strategies that can be mixed and matched.
whatIBuilt: Hex-grid Reversi engine with configurable board shapes and starting states
MVC architecture with GUI + text views and a controller layer
Strategy system including minimax and composable heuristics
howItWorks: Represent the board on an odd-r hex coordinate system
Validate moves and apply flips according to Reversi rules
Run players (human/AI) through a controller loop
Plug different strategies into the AI player
nextSteps: Add stronger evaluation heuristics and time controls
Improve UI polish and strategy visualization
bullets: Reversi on a hexagonal odd-r grid with configurable board shapes and starting states.
Extensible MVC design with pluggable views, controllers, and player types.
Modular strategies support minimax and composable heuristic evaluation.

title: Linux Shell (C)
oneLiner: Custom C shell with pipelines, signal handling, and a suite of built-in commands.
cardDescription: Custom C shell supporting pipelines, redirection, signals, and built-in commands.
proofPoints: Pipelines + redirection + signal handling
Built-ins: source, prev, cd, help, verbose
Process orchestration for sequential execution
overview: A custom shell written in C with support for command execution, piping, redirection, and signals. The project focuses on process control and correctness under typical shell behaviors.
whatIBuilt: Command execution with piping and I/O redirection
Signal handling and process orchestration
Built-in commands (source, prev, cd, help, verbose toggle)
howItWorks: Parse input into commands and pipeline segments
Fork/exec child processes and wire pipes between them
Apply redirections and handle signals appropriately
Track history for built-ins like prev
nextSteps: Add job control (fg/bg) if desired
Harden parsing for edge cases and quoting rules
bullets: Fully operational shell with commands, piping, redirection, and signal handling.
Uses child processes and process orchestration to support standard shell behavior.
Includes custom built-ins like source, prev, cd, help, and verbose mode.
